channel.title: Microsoft Learn - in Intune
channel.description: The latest updates from Microsoft Learn in Intune

item.title: Add and Assign Win32 Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add, assign, and manage Win32 apps with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deploy Windows Update Packages in Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to deploy a Windows update package (.msu file) in Intune.

item.title: Add the Company Portal for macOS App - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add the macOS Company Portal app.

item.title: Add an Unmanaged macOS PKG App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add an unmanaged macOS PKG app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add a Windows Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add a Windows line-of-business (LOB) app using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add an Enterprise App Catalog App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to add an Enterprise App Catalog app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: How to Add macOS Line-of-Business Apps to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add macOS line-of-business (LOB) apps to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add a macOS DMG App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add a macOS DMG app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add an iOS/iPadOS Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add an iOS/iPadOS line-of-business (LOB) app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Add an Android Line-of-Business App to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about how to add a Android line-of-business (LOB) app to Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Configure Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with Intune and onboard devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Connect Microsoft Defender for Endpoint to Intune, onboard devices by platform, and configure compliance and Conditional Access policies that use device risk levels to control access to corporate resources.

item.title: Use Intune Suite add-on capabilities - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune Suite unifies a series of mission-critical advanced endpoint management and security capabilities. The capabilities of the suite are integrated with Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Security across endpoint platforms for both cloud and on-premises co-managed devices.

item.title: Learn about using Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: To enhance the security of your organization, set your users to run with standard permissions while Endpoint Privilege Management ensures those users can seamlessly run specified files with elevated rights.

item.title: Encrypt Windows devices with BitLocker using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to manage BitLocker encryption on Windows devices, including silent encryption and Personal Data Encryption.

item.title: Device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Overview of Microsoft Intune device compliance, including tenant-wide compliance policy settings and device compliance policies.

item.title: In development - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes Microsoft Intune features that are in development.

item.title: Operating systems and browsers supported by Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Lists supported device platforms and browsers for Intune device management

item.title: Mobile Application Management (MAM) for unenrolled devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use mobile application management without enrollment to deploy apps, and protect organization data within the apps. Get an overview of the administrator and end user tasks for this enrollment option.

item.title: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create assignment filters in Microsoft Intune to target policies based on device properties like OS version or manufacturer. Learn to create, update, and delete filters for managed devices and apps.

item.title: Deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Follow the steps to deploy Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Manage approved apps for Windows devices with App Control for Business policy and Managed Installers in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use App Control for Business policies and a managed installer to manage which apps are approved to run on Windows devices that you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Planning guide to move to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan, design, implement, adopt, and move to Microsoft Intune. Get guidance and advice to determine goals, use-case scenarios and requirements, and create rollout and communication plans, support, testing, and validation plans.

item.title: Set up Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Step 1 for deploying or setting up Intune. The starting point is to review supported configurations, sign up for the trial, configure the custom domain name, add users and groups to Intune, assign licenses to users, manage roles, grant admin permissions, and set the MDM authority.

item.title: Plan for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the requirements and capabilities of Remote Help with Microsoft Intune on Windows, macOS, and Android Enterprise.

item.title: Deploy Intune policies to manage Windows LAPS - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create policies to manage the Windows Local Administrator Policy Solution (LAPS) on Windows devices that enrolled with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidelines for using Azure Virtual Desktop multi-session with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags for distributed IT - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use role-based access control (RBAC) and scope tags to filter configuration profiles to specific roles.

item.title: Migration guide to Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deployment guide to set up, onboard, or move to Intune. These steps include moving from partner MDM providers, using co-management, moving from on-premises group policy, and moving from Office 365 device management.

item.title: Find the primary user of a Microsoft Intune device. - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Find the primary user (or User Device Affinity) of an Intune device.

item.title: Sign Up for Microsoft Intune Free Trial Setup Guide - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Sign up for a Microsoft Intune free trial and configure your tenant. Learn the setup process, prerequisites, and how to configure domain names. Start evaluating Intune today.

item.title: Windows compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of all the settings you can use when setting compliance for your Windows, Windows Holographic, and Surface Hub devices in Microsoft Intune. Check for compliance on the minimum and maximum operating system, set password restrictions and length, check for partner anti-virus (AV) solutions, enable encryption on data storage, and more.

item.title: Tutorial - Walkthrough the Microsoft Intune Admin Center - Microsoft Intune
item.description: In this tutorial, you will tour Microsoft Intune in the Microsoft Intune admin center to better understand how to accomplish tasks.

item.title: Learn about Intune security baselines for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that have preset and recommended configurations to the Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Reports - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Intune provides specific report types with focused views that contain consistent and timely data.

item.title: Encrypt macOS devices with FileVault using Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune policy to configure and manage FileVault disk encryption on macOS devices, including Setup Assistant enforcement and comprehensive recovery key management.

item.title: Configure compliance policies with actions for noncompliance in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure your compliance policies with one or more actions for noncompliance to protect devices and your organization from unprotected devices. Actions can remotely lock devices, send email or notifications to device users, and more.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage macOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Get started with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See an overview of the steps to start using Intune. Plan your move and deployment of Intune, determine your licensing needs and any platform requirements, use compliance and Conditional Access, deploy apps, create device configuration profiles, and enroll your devices to be managed. Get more information on mobile application management for BYOD or personal devices.

item.title: Manage firewall settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure and deploy policies for devices you manage with endpoint security firewall policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: A guide to managing iOS/iPadOS devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Assignment filter properties and operators reference - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Reference guide for device and app properties, operators, and rule syntax when creating assignment filters in Microsoft Intune. Includes examples and supported values.

item.title: Plan and Prepare for Endpoint Privilege Management Deployment - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Plan your Endpoint Privilege Management deploying by understanding requirements, fundamentals, and security recommendations.

item.title: Audit changes and events in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to review audit logs that record Microsoft Intune activities.

item.title: Manage and secure devices overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing devices that access organization resources in Microsoft Intune. You can manage new and existing devices, including BYOD personal devices, check health compliance and view reports, configure device features, and secure devices using mobile threat solutions.

item.title: Manage account protection settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Microsoft Intune endpoint security account protection policies to protect the identity and accounts of users.

item.title: Create device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create device compliance policies for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Device Query - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use device query in Microsoft Intune to get on-demand device state, run Kusto Query Language (KQL) queries, and troubleshoot devices.

item.title: Add groups to organize users and devices for Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Create Microsoft Entra groups to organize users and devices for use with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: View device details with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View your device details, including operating systems, storage space, manufacturer, and model. Get a list of installed apps, check compliance policies, and set up TeamViewer with Microsoft Intune in Azure. Similar to viewing inventory of the devices you manage.

item.title: Creating elevation rules with Endpoint Privilege Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View guidance on how to create strong file elevation rules with Microsoft Intune Endpoint Privilege Management

item.title: Configure security baseline policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Deploy security baselines that establish a default and recommended security postures on Windows devices you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Monitor results of your device compliance policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the device compliance dashboard to understand overall device compliance the per policy and per setting device compliance results.

item.title: Manage endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Security Administrators can use the Endpoint Security policies and profiles to focus on security configuration of devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Using Azure Virtual Desktop single-session with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Guidelines for using Azure Virtual Desktop single-session with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Managing Elevation Settings for Endpoint Privilege Management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View guidance on how to manage the Endpoint Privilege Management client, including reporting level and default elevation response.

item.title: Deployment guide to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn the recommended processes to manage Android devices in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Android Enterprise compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of all the settings you can use when setting compliance for your Android Enterprise devices in Microsoft Intune. Set password rules, choose a minimum or maximum operating system version, restrict specific apps, prevent reusing password, and more.

item.title: iOS/iPadOS device compliance settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the device compliance settings for iOS/iPadOS that you can manage with Microsoft Intune compliance policies.

item.title: Device Query for Multiple Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use device query for multiple devices in Microsoft Intune to run KQL queries, analyze inventory trends, and start querying your managed fleet now.

item.title: Microsoft Intune Government Service overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn more about the Intune government service offerings and features. This article is designed to serve as an overview of the Microsoft Intune offering for government community cloud (GCC) High and United States Department of Defense (DoD) environments.

item.title: Scenarios for using Conditional Access with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how Conditional Access is commonly used with Intune compliance policy for devices and apps

item.title: Assignment filter reports & troubleshooting - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use assignment filter reports to troubleshoot policies in Microsoft Intune. View filter evaluation results, resolve conflicts, and understand include vs. exclude modes.

item.title: Using Windows virtual machines with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article describes the general guidelines for using Windows 10/11 virtual machines with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Manage and secure user and group identities overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing identities in Microsoft Intune. Use existing users and groups, control access using RBAC, establish user affinity, and secure and authenticate users.

item.title: Protect data and devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the Intune capabilities that can help you protect your devices and data against unauthorized access and other threats.

item.title: Manage and secure apps overview - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Get an overview of the concepts and features you should know when managing apps that access organization resources in Microsoft Intune. You can deploy apps used by your organization, including Microsoft Edge and Microsoft 365. You can also configure apps, protect apps on organizations owned and BYOD personal devices, and update apps that you deploy.

item.title: Add, Configure, and Protect Apps with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Add, configure, and protect apps with Intune.

item.title: View report details for encryption status of devices managed with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the Microsoft Intune admin center to view reports for device encryption status across macOS FileVault and Windows BitLocker encrypted devices that you manage with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Use custom compliance settings for Linux and Windows devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage custom compliance settings for Linux and Windows devices by using JSON files and discovery scripts in Intune.

item.title: Zero Trust with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Microsoft Intune contributes to a strong Zero Trust strategy and architecture by managing and securing endpoints that access organizational resources.

item.title: Settings list for the Microsoft Intune security baseline for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the settings in the Microsoft Intune security baseline for Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and each settings default value.

item.title: Use app-based Conditional Access policies with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about using app-based Conditional Access policies with Microsoft Intune for both enrolled and unenrolled devices.

item.title: Settings you can manage with Intune Endpoint Protection profiles for Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View the available settings in Intune endpoint protection profiles for managed Windows devices.

item.title: Configure compliance policies - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Description for configuring compliance policies

item.title: Manage antivirus settings with endpoint security policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure and deploy policies and use reports for devices you manage with endpoint security antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Network endpoints for US government deployments - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See the list of US government endpoint URLs that Intune needs and requires. Allow the ports, IP addresses, and endpoint URLs in your proxy server configuration.

item.title: Use EPM support approvals for file elevation requests with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage support approvals for elevation requests when you use Endpoint Privilege Management for Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deploy Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand the steps and phases for deploying Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Send Email Notifications to Noncompliant Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to create notification templates and send email alerts to users with noncompliant devices in Microsoft Intune. Configure actions for noncompliance.

item.title: Policy Sets - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use policy sets to group collections of management objects in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Windows Antivirus policy settings for Microsoft Defender Antivirus for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: See a list of the settings in the Microsoft Defender Antivirus profile for Windows devices. You can configure these settings as part of Endpoint security Antivirus policy in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deploy policy for Windows Hello to groups of Windows devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use a Microsoft Intune profile for Identity protection configure Windows Hello for Business on Windows devices.

item.title: Mobile Threat Defense with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use Intune Mobile Threat Defense (MTD) with your Mobile Threat Defense partner to protect access to company resources based on device risk.

item.title: Remotely administer devices in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Install the previous TeamViewer connector to remotely administer devices using Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Deployment guide for Windows device management - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use our platform deployment guide to set up Windows device management in Microsoft Intune.

item.title: Known Issues for Endpoint Privilege Management with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Configure policies that define how Endpoint Privilege Management functions in your tenant, and behaviors when elevating files to run in administrative context.

item.title: Windows Hello for Business settings in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: View details for Windows Hello for Business settings you configure in an Intune identity protection profile for device groups in Intune.

item.title: Troubleshoot and monitor Remote Help for Microsoft Intune. - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the troubleshooting and monitoring information for Remote Help with Microsoft Intune to ensure healthy and successful deployment.

item.title: Intune endpoint security firewall settings - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Endpoint security firewall policy settings for Windows and macOS in Microsoft Intune

item.title: Use reusable groups of settings policies in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Manage groups of settings for Intune profiles as a single object and then add that settings group object to multiple profile instances. Later changes you make to the settings groups automatically apply to each profile that includes the reusable settings group.

item.title: Protection and configuration levels overview in Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the different levels of protection and configuration in Microsoft Intune, including minimum, enhanced, and high levels. Understand the recommended policies for apps, compliance, and device configuration at each level to secure your organization's resources effectively.

item.title: Resource Performance Report - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Use the resource performance report in Intune to analyze CPU and RAM trends, identify at-risk devices, and act on recommendations to improve user experience.

item.title: Assignment Filter Performance Tips for Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Optimize Microsoft Intune performance with assignment filters. Learn to use virtual groups, reuse groups, and apply filters effectively. Improve policy deployment speed with incremental group changes, and use assignment filters to include and exclude.

item.title: Manage Specialty devices with Microsoft Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: This article provides information about specialty devices and how can you manage them with Microsoft Intune

item.title: Use Endpoint Privilege Management to transition users from administrator to standard user - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Understand the steps and phases for using Endpoint Privilege Management to transition users from administrators to standard users.

item.title: Android Open Source Project Supported Devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Lists Android open source project devices (AOSP) supported devices

item.title: Tutorial - Use Microsoft Intune to protect Exchange Online email from unmanaged iOS devices - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn how to use Microsoft Intune app protection policies and Conditional Access to prevent unmanaged iOS devices from accessing Exchange Online.

item.title: Help Desk Troubleshooting Dashboard - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Help desk staff use the troubleshooting pane to solve users' technical problems.

item.title: Distributed IT with multiple admins - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about scaling guidelines for Microsoft Intune when you have a large number of local admins who need to manage their own users/devices and policies within the same tenant. Use Microsoft Intune's Role Based Access Control to manage access.

item.title: Manage device operating system versions with Intune - Microsoft Intune
item.description: Learn about the methods for managing device operating system versions supported by Microsoft Intune.
